title: Review this uncommitted diff for bugs, security issues, and code quality. Be concise (max 10 lines).
summary_text: - High: pure quote-URL posts will now fail hard. [`x_poster.py:69`](file:///Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/x_poster.py#L69) rejects empty `text`, but [`scheduler.py:510`](file:///Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L510) strips the URL before calling `post_tweet(..., quote_tweet_id=...)`; if the post body is just the URL (or URL + whitespace), this becomes `ValueError` and the scheduler never posts. - Medium: Twitter URL detection is incomplete/inconsistent. [`scheduler.py:504`](file:///Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L504) and [`scheduler.py:268`](file:///Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L268) only check `twitter.com/status/`, but real tweet URLs are usually `twitter.com/<user>/status/<id>`. Those posts will be misclassified, likely requiring an image at approval time and skipping quote-tweet behavior at send time. - Medium: `has_quote_url` is broader than the extractor. [`scheduler.py:504`](file:///Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L504) treats any `x.com/` URL as quote-like, but [`x_poster.py:14`](file:///Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/x_poster.py#L14) only extracts `/status/<id>` URLs. Non-status `x.com` links will silently fall back to plain text posting while still suppressing the image path requirement, which is a behavior mismatch. - No obvious new security issue in this diff; main gaps are posting reliability and branch-condition drift.
